core_meaning: Past tense with avoir expressing completed past actions and achieved results with regular and most verbs.
primary_communicative_fn: Describing completed past actions, events, and results in spoken French.
when_to_use: Use for any completed past action with regular and most verbs; most common past tense in spoken French.
pattern: j'ai [past participle] / tu as [pp] / il a [pp] / nous avons [pp] / vous avez [pp] / ils ont [pp]
key_variations: Agreement: past participle agrees with preceding direct object (la fille que j'ai vue); negation: je n'ai pas [pp]; question: est-ce que tu as [pp]?
essential_vocabulary_slots: participes réguliers: parlé, mangé, fini, choisi, vendu, pris | expressions temporelles: hier, la semaine dernière, ce matin, récemment | actions: mangé, bu, fait, vu, dit, écrit, dormi
common_learner_mistakes: Forgetting accent on ai in j'ai; using être instead of avoir for motion verbs; not applying participle agreement with preceding object.
chunk_compatibility: j'ai parlé, tu as mangé, il a fini, nous avons choisi, vous avez vu, ils ont pris
key_forms: j'ai, tu as, il/elle a, nous avons, vous avez, ils/elles ont + participe passé
essential_vocabulary: ai, as, a, avons, avez, ont, parlé, mangé, vu, fait, dit, bu, écrit
why_it_matters: Most common past tense in spoken French; essential for narrating events and recent completed actions.
common_mistakes: Using être instead of avoir; forgetting participle agreement; wrong past participles; pronunciation issues.

core_meaning: Past tense with être expressing movement, state changes, and reflexive actions in the past.
primary_communicative_fn: Describing movement, arrivals, departures, and reflexive past actions.
when_to_use: Use with 14 motion verbs (aller, venir, partir, entrer, sortir, arriver, rester, monter, descendre, retourner, naître, mourir) and all reflexive verbs.
pattern: je suis [past participle] / tu es [pp] / il est [pp] / nous sommes [pp] / vous êtes [pp] / ils sont [pp] (+ agreement with subject)
key_variations: Agreement: participle agrees with subject (elle est venue, ils sont venus); feminine forms: allé→allée, venu→venue; reflexive: je me suis levé
essential_vocabulary_slots: verbes de mouvement: aller, venir, partir, arriver, entrer, sortir, monter, descendre, retourner | changements d'état: naître, mourir, rester | réfléchis: se lever, s'habiller, se coucher, se réveiller
common_learner_mistakes: Forgetting participle agreement with subject (ils sont venus not ils est venu); using avoir with motion verbs; confusing with avoir-auxiliary verbs.
chunk_compatibility: je suis allé, tu es parti, il est arrivé, nous sommes venus, vous êtes entrés, ils sont sortis
primary_function: Movement and state change in past
key_forms: je suis, tu es, il/elle est, nous sommes, vous êtes, ils/elles sont + participe passé
essential_vocabulary: suis, es, est, sommes, êtes, sont, allé, parti, arrivé, venu, entré, sorti
why_it_matters: Essential for narrating movement, arrivals, and all reflexive verbs in the past; agreement is critical.
common_mistakes: Forgetting subject agreement; using avoir instead; confusing motion verbs requiring être; wrong participles.

core_meaning: Subject pronouns indicating who performs the action (I, you, he, she, we, they).
primary_communicative_fn: Identifying the performer of actions and subjects of sentences.
when_to_use: Use subject pronouns to indicate who performs the action. French requires explicit subject pronouns unlike Spanish.
pattern: je / tu / il, elle, on / nous / vous / ils, elles
key_variations: On = we/one (informal we); il/elle for 'it'; stressed forms: moi, toi, lui, elle, nous, vous, eux, elles; inversion in questions: ai-je?
essential_vocabulary_slots: singular: je, tu, il, elle, on | plural: nous, vous, ils, elles | toniques: moi, toi, lui, elle, nous, vous, eux, elles | prépositions: pour moi, avec toi
common_learner_mistakes: Dropping subject pronouns (required in French); confusing on with nous in speech; using object pronouns as subjects.
chunk_compatibility: je suis, tu es, il va, elle parle, on mange, nous parlons, vous faites, ils viennent
key_forms: je, tu, il, elle, on, nous, vous, ils, elles
essential_vocabulary: je, tu, il, elle, on, nous, vous, ils, elles, moi, toi, lui, nous, vous, eux
why_it_matters: Foundation of French grammar; subject pronouns are mandatory and identify the actor in every sentence.
common_mistakes: Dropping subject pronouns; confusing on/nous; using object pronouns; wrong tu/vous distinction.

core_meaning: Direct object pronouns replacing things directly acted upon (me, you, him, her, it, us, them).
primary_communicative_fn: Replacing direct objects to avoid repetition and increase fluency.
when_to_use: Use to replace things directly acted upon (see it, help him, call her). Placed before verb in simple tenses, before auxiliary in compound.
pattern: me (m') / te (t') / le, la (l') / nous / vous / les (placed before verb)
key_variations: Before vowel: m', t', l'; before past participle: je l'ai vu; negative: je ne le vois pas; imperative affirmative: vois-le!
essential_vocabulary_slots: replacements: me, myself | te, yourself | le, him/it (masc) | la, her/it (fem) | nous, us | vous, yourselves | les, them | objects: le livre, la maison, l'ami
common_learner_mistakes: Placing direct object after verb (wrong word order); forgetting liaison l'+ vowel; confusing le (object) with lui (indirect).
chunk_compatibility: je le vois, tu la vois, il l'aime, nous les voyons, vous m'aidez, ils te parlent
key_forms: me, te, le, la, nous, vous, les
essential_vocabulary: me, te, le, la, l', nous, vous, les, voir, aimer, aider, appeler, chercher
why_it_matters: Essential for fluent speech avoiding repetition; correct placement (before verb) is critical for comprehension.
common_mistakes: Wrong word order; confusing direct/indirect objects; forgetting liaison; wrong object forms.

core_meaning: Indirect object pronouns replacing recipients and beneficiaries (to me, to him, to her, to us, to them).
primary_communicative_fn: Replacing indirect objects to avoid repetition while maintaining sentence flow.
when_to_use: Use for persons to whom an action is directed (give TO, tell TO, speak TO). Preceded by à in full form.
pattern: me (m') / te (t') / lui / nous / vous / leur (placed before verb)
key_variations: Before vowel: m', t'; with infinitive: je vais lui parler; past tense: je lui ai parlé; negation: je ne lui parle pas
essential_vocabulary_slots: replacements: me, to me | te, to you | lui, to him/to her | nous, to us | vous, to you | leur, to them | full: à moi, à toi, à lui, à elle
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing direct (le/la) with indirect (lui/leur); using à + noun where pronoun needed; wrong verb agreement.
chunk_compatibility: je lui parle, tu lui dis, il nous aide, nous vous expliquons, vous leur montrez, ils me donnent
key_forms: me, te, lui, nous, vous, leur
essential_vocabulary: me, te, lui, nous, vous, leur, à moi, à toi, à lui, à elle, parler, donner
why_it_matters: Essential distinction from direct objects; correct indirect object use prevents ambiguity in communication.
common_mistakes: Confusing direct/indirect; using noun instead of pronoun; wrong pronoun forms; placement errors.

core_meaning: Stressed pronouns used for emphasis, after prepositions, and in comparisons (me, you, him, her, us, them).
primary_communicative_fn: Emphasizing, clarifying, and contrasting subjects; required after prepositions.
when_to_use: Use after prepositions (pour, avec, sans, chez), for emphasis, after c'est, in comparisons, and to clarify subjects.
pattern: moi / toi / lui / elle / nous / vous / eux / elles
key_variations: With self: moi-même, toi-même, lui-même; after c'est: c'est moi, c'est toi; with prepositions: pour moi, avec eux
essential_vocabulary_slots: prepositions: pour, avec, sans, contre, chez, sans, devant, derrière | emphasis: moi je, toi tu, lui il | comparisons: moi aussi, toi non, lui non plus
common_learner_mistakes: Using subject pronouns after prepositions (wrong: pour je→pour moi); forgetting emphasis form; confusing with object pronouns.
chunk_compatibility: pour moi, avec toi, sans lui, chez elle, c'est moi, c'est toi, moi je, toi tu
key_forms: moi, toi, lui, elle, nous, vous, eux, elles
essential_vocabulary: moi, toi, lui, elle, nous, vous, eux, elles, pour, avec, sans, chez
why_it_matters: Essential after prepositions and for emphasis; distinguish from subject and object pronouns.
common_mistakes: Using subject pronouns after prepositions; wrong pronoun forms; missing emphasis construction.

core_meaning: Demonstrative pronouns replacing demonstrative adjectives without repetition (this one, that one, these, those).
primary_communicative_fn: Pointing to specific things and avoiding repetition of already-identified nouns.
when_to_use: Use demonstrative pronouns to replace nouns already mentioned. They agree in gender and number with what they replace.
pattern: celui / celle / ceux / celles (+ -ci/-là for close/distant) / ce (celui qui, ce que)
key_variations: celui-ci (this one), celui-là (that one); feminine: celle-ci, celle-là; plural: ceux-ci, ceux-là; with relative: celui qui, celui que
essential_vocabulary_slots: pointing: celui-ci, celui-là, celle-ci, celle-là | choices: lequel, laquelle, lesquels, lesquelles | relations: celui de, celle de
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing demonstrative pronouns (celui) with demonstrative adjectives (ce, cette); forgetting -ci/-là; wrong agreement.
chunk_compatibility: celui-ci, celui-là, celui qui, celui que, ce qui, ce que, ceux-là, celles-ci
key_forms: celui, celle, ceux, celles, celui-ci, celui-là, ce, qui, que
essential_vocabulary: celui, celle, ceux, celles, celui-ci, celui-là, ce, qui, que, lequel, laquelle
why_it_matters: Essential for avoiding noun repetition and clarifying which specific person or thing is meant.
common_mistakes: Confusing pronouns with adjectives; forgetting -ci/-là; wrong gender agreement; missing relative clause.

core_meaning: Possessive pronouns replacing possessive adjective + noun to avoid repetition (mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs).
primary_communicative_fn: Replacing possessive constructions to avoid repetition while maintaining clarity about ownership.
when_to_use: Use possessive pronouns after être or to replace noun + possessive structure. They agree with the possessed object, not the possessor.
pattern: le mien / la mienne / les miens / les miennes / le tien / la tienne / le sien / le nôtre / le vôtre / le leur
key_variations: Singular masc: le mien, le tien, le sien, le nôtre, le vôtre, le leur; singular fem: la mienne, la tienne; plural: les miens, les tiens
essential_vocabulary_slots: mine: le mien, la mienne, les miens, les miennes | yours: le tien, la tienne, les tiens, les tiennes | his/her: le sien, la sienne | ours: le nôtre | theirs: le leur
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing with possessive adjectives (mon, ma, mes); agreement errors (le sien not la sien); using with noun (wrong: mon livre le mien).
chunk_compatibility: le mien, la mienne, le tien, la tienne, le sien, la sienne, le nôtre, le vôtre, le leur
key_forms: le mien, la mienne, les miens, les miennes, le tien, le sien, le nôtre, le vôtre, le leur
essential_vocabulary: mien, mienne, miens, miennes, tien, sien, nôtre, vôtre, leur
why_it_matters: Essential for avoiding possessive adjective repetition; correct agreement prevents ambiguity.
common_mistakes: Confusing with possessive adjectives; wrong agreement; using with noun; missing circumflex accent.

core_meaning: Demonstrative adjectives pointing to specific nouns and indicating proximity or distance (this, that, these, those).
primary_communicative_fn: Identifying and pointing to specific nouns; distinguishing close versus distant items with -ci/-là.
when_to_use: Use demonstrative adjectives before nouns to point to specific things. Add -ci or -là to show distance when needed.
pattern: ce / cet / cette / ces (+ -ci/-là for emphasis)
key_variations: cet before masc singular vowel: cet homme, cet ami; ce before masc consonant: ce livre; cette for all feminine; ces for all plurals; proximity: ce livre-ci, cette maison-là
essential_vocabulary_slots: masc consonne: ce livre, ce chien, ce chat | masc voyelle: cet homme, cet ami, cet océan | fém sing: cette femme, cette voiture | pluriel: ces enfants, ces fleurs
common_learner_mistakes: Using ces before singular nouns; forgetting cet before vowels; wrong gender; confusing with demonstrative pronouns.
chunk_compatibility: ce livre, cet homme, cette maison, ces enfants, ce livre-ci, cette fille-là
key_forms: ce, cet, cette, ces, ce...ci, ce...là
essential_vocabulary: ce, cet, cette, ces, -ci, -là, livre, homme, maison, enfants, ami, océan
why_it_matters: Foundation for pointing to specific items; essential for everyday reference and making comparisons.
common_mistakes: Using ces with singular; forgetting cet; wrong gender; confusing with pronouns; omitting -ci/-là.

core_meaning: Possessive adjectives showing ownership and relationships between possessor and possessed (my, your, his, her, our, their).
primary_communicative_fn: Indicating ownership, kinship, and relationships between people and possessions.
when_to_use: Use possessive adjectives before nouns to show ownership or relationship. They agree with the possessed object, not the possessor.
key_variations: mon/ma/mes (my), ton/ta/tes (your sing), son/sa/ses (his/her/its), notre/votre/leur (our/your pl/their); plural forms: nos, vos, leurs; agree with noun gender
essential_vocabulary_slots: singular: mon père, ma mère, mon enfant, ta maison, son chat, sa voiture, ses clés | plural: mes parents, nos enfants, vos amis, leurs livres
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing son/sa/ses (his/her) with English his/her (English marks person, French marks noun); wrong agreement (sa livre should be son livre).
chunk_compatibility: mon père, ma mère, mon enfant, ta maison, son chat, sa voiture, nos enfants, vos amis
key_forms: mon, ma, mes, ton, ta, tes, son, sa, ses, notre, votre, leur
essential_vocabulary: mon, ma, mes, ton, ta, tes, son, sa, ses, notre, votre, leur, père, mère, maison
why_it_matters: Foundation for expressing relationships and ownership; agreement with noun gender is critical and differs from English.
common_mistakes: Using wrong gender (confusing person's gender with noun gender); forgetting agreement; using with article.

core_meaning: Feminine form of adjectives showing gender agreement with feminine nouns and female subjects.
primary_communicative_fn: Making adjectives agree with feminine nouns and subjects in gender.
when_to_use: Use feminine adjective form when modifying feminine singular nouns or female subjects. Most add -e, some have special forms.
pattern: masculine → feminine: + -e / doubled consonant + -e / -x → -se / irregular forms
key_variations: Regular: grand→grande, petit→petite; doubled: bon→bonne, fin→fine; -x→-se: heureux→heureuse; irregular: blanc→blanche, long→longue
essential_vocabulary_slots: regular: grand(e), petit(e), beau(belle), nouveau(nouvelle), intelligent(e) | doubles: bon(ne), ancien(ne), cruel(le) | -x→-se: heureux(euse), sérieux(euse) | colors: blanc(he), vert(e)
common_learner_mistakes: Forgetting feminine -e on adjectives after feminine nouns; not applying agreement with female subjects (elle est grand→grande).
chunk_compatibility: une femme grande, une maison belle, une fille intelligente, une voiture nouvelle, une personne hereuse
key_forms: grande, petite, bonne, nouvelle, intelligente, heureuse, blanche, verte
essential_vocabulary: grand, belle, petit, nouveau, intelligent, heureux, blanc, vert, bonne, cruelle
why_it_matters: Adjective agreement is mandatory in French; feminine forms distinguish from masculine in writing and pronunciation.
common_mistakes: Forgetting -e endings; not applying with female subjects; irregular forms; confusion with masculines.

core_meaning: Plural form of adjectives showing number agreement with plural nouns and subjects.
primary_communicative_fn: Making adjectives agree with plural nouns in number.
when_to_use: Use plural adjective form when modifying plural nouns or subjects. Most add -s, some add -x, irregular forms exist.
key_variations: Regular: grand→grands, petite→petites; -x ending: heureux→heureux (no change); -al: international→internationaux; invariable: bleu marine, rouge bordeaux
essential_vocabulary_slots: regular: petit(s), grand(s), nouveau(x), beau(x), intelligent(s) | -x: heureux(ses), délicieux(ses), mystérieux(ses) | -al→-aux: national(aux), international(aux) | colors: bleu(s), vert(s)
common_learner_mistakes: Forgetting -s on plural adjectives; using -x where -s needed; irregular -al plural (internationaux not internationals).
chunk_compatibility: des hommes grands, des femmes intelligentes, des enfants heureux, des voitures rouges
key_forms: grands, petites, beaux, nouveaux, intelligents, heureux
essential_vocabulary: grand, petit, nouveau, beau, heureux, intelligent, national, bleu, rouge, vert
why_it_matters: Plural agreement is mandatory in French; marks plurality in agreement chains throughout noun phrases.
common_mistakes: Omitting -s; using wrong plural ending; incorrect -al→-aux transformation; invariable colors.

core_meaning: Complete adjective agreement in gender (masculine/feminine) and number (singular/plural) with nouns.
primary_communicative_fn: Ensuring adjectives match the gender and number of nouns they modify.
when_to_use: All adjectives in French must agree in gender and number with the noun they modify (four possible forms per adjective).
pattern: [masculine singular] → [feminine singular] / [masculine plural] / [feminine plural] with pattern: + -e, + -s, + -es, or irregular
key_variations: Four forms: -Ø (masc sg), -e (fem sg), -s (masc pl), -es (fem pl); bon→bon, bonne, bons, bonnes; compound: petit(e), bleu(e)
essential_vocabulary_slots: regular: blanc(blanche, blancs, blanches), grand(grande, grands, grandes) | nationality: portugais(portugaise, portugais, portugaises) | profession: avocat(avocate, avocats, avocates)
common_learner_mistakes: Only changing gender but not number; confusing with English where adjectives don't change; not applying consistently.
chunk_compatibility: un homme intelligent, une femme intelligente, des hommes intelligents, des femmes intelligentes
key_forms: masculine singular, feminine singular, masculine plural, feminine plural
essential_vocabulary: accord, genre, nombre, masculin, féminin, singulier, pluriel
why_it_matters: Complete agreement (gender AND number) is mandatory in French and impacts written and spoken correctness.
common_mistakes: Partial agreement; forgetting number; confusing with other languages; incorrect irregular forms.

core_meaning: Unchanging adjectives that remain the same in all gender and number contexts (invariable colors and compounds).
primary_communicative_fn: Using adjectives that never change form regardless of noun's gender or number.
when_to_use: Use certain adjectives that never change: most colors from nouns (marron, orange) and some compound adjectives.
pattern: adjective remains unchanged regardless of masculine/feminine or singular/plural
key_variations: Colors from nouns: marron, orange, beige, kaki, indigo, pourpre; compound colors: bleu marine, rouge bordeaux; some: fini, possible
essential_vocabulary_slots: colors: marron, orange, beige, kaki, indigo, pourpre | compound colors: bleu marine, rouge bordeaux, vert olive | others: possible, fini, super
common_learner_mistakes: Adding -s or -e to invariable colors (des voitures orange not oranges); thinking all colors work this way (some do change).
chunk_compatibility: une voiture marron, des chemises orange, des yeux bleu marine, des robes rouge bordeaux
key_forms: marron, orange, beige, kaki, indigo, pourpre, bleu marine, rouge bordeaux
essential_vocabulary: marron, orange, beige, kaki, indigo, pourpre, marine, bordeaux, olive
why_it_matters: Recognizing invariable adjectives prevents adding incorrect agreement markers in speech and writing.
common_mistakes: Adding endings to color nouns used as adjectives; overgeneralizing to all colors.

core_meaning: Compound adjectives (two-part and complex forms) with special agreement rules and hyphenated structures.
primary_communicative_fn: Using compound adjectives formed from multiple words with special agreement patterns.
when_to_use: Use compound adjectives formed by two words (often hyphenated) with specific agreement rules for each type.
pattern: grand-mère, petit-fils / compound colors: bleu marine / special structures with unique pluralization
key_variations: Family: grand-mère→grands-mères (both change), petit-fils→petits-fils; colors: bleu marine (both invariable); some compounds follow special rules
essential_vocabulary_slots: family: grand-mère, grand-père, petit-fils, petit-fille, belle-fille, beau-père | compound colors: bleu marine, rouge bordeaux | special: nouveau-né, État-major
common_learner_mistakes: Making compound adjectives agree like regular ones (grand-mères not grand-mère for plural); confusing which compounds follow which rules.
chunk_compatibility: grand-mère, grands-parents, petit-fils, belle-mère, bleu marine, rouge bordeaux
key_forms: grand-mère, grands-mères, petit-fils, petits-fils, bleu marine, rouge bordeaux
essential_vocabulary: grand, mère, père, petit, fils, fille, belle, bleu, marine, rouge, bordeaux
why_it_matters: Family terms and compound colors are high-frequency; learning their specific plurals prevents errors.
common_mistakes: Wrong plural forms; applying regular agreement to invariable compounds; confusion with singular/plural rules.
